name: Western Catskills Community Revitalization Council, Inc
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit providing financial counseling, housing rehabilitation, and community revitalization services across rural New York since 1981.
description_long:
Western Catskills Community Revitalization Council, Inc. was incorporated in 1981 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization operating as part of New York State Homes and Community Renewal's Rural Preservation Program. Over 45 years, the organization has assisted 1,514 households and directed over $31.9 million into its service communities across Delaware, Greene, and Schoharie counties in rural upstate New York.

WCCRC offers HUD-certified financial, housing, and mortgage counseling alongside comprehensive housing rehabilitation programs, affordable senior and disabled housing units, business training and assistance, grant writing and administration, and civic community presentations. Their counseling services are delivered by certified professionals meeting federal standards for consumer financial guidance.

What distinguishes WCCRC is its integrated approach combining counseling with direct community development—they don't just advise clients but actively manage affordable housing units, administer grants, and facilitate commercial improvements. Their deep roots in rural preservation reflect a specialized focus on underserved communities where traditional financial services are limited, with explicit commitments to equity, access, and partnership with local municipalities.

The organization operates transparently with stated values around stewardship and community empowerment. However, as a geographically limited nonprofit, their services are only available in specific rural New York towns, and housing assistance involves waiting lists. Their counseling services appear to be free or low-cost (typical of HUD-certified nonprofits), but detailed pricing structures are not publicly detailed on the website.
pros: HUD-certified financial, housing, and mortgage counseling meeting federal consumer protection standards
45 years of demonstrated community impact with $31.9+ million invested locally and 1,514 households assisted
Integrated services combining counseling with housing rehabilitation and property management for holistic support
Explicitly serves low-income, senior, and disabled populations with dedicated affordable housing programs
Operates as transparent 501(c)(3) nonprofit with stated values around equity, access, and community partnership
Direct relationship with NY State Homes and Community Renewal's Rural Preservation Program ensuring legitimacy
Offers business training and entrepreneurship support beyond housing, addressing broader economic stability
cons: Services limited to 16 specific rural towns in Delaware, Greene, and Schoharie counties—not accessible statewide or nationally
Housing and apartment programs operate on waiting lists with no published timeline for access
Website does not clearly specify pricing, fees, or eligibility requirements for counseling services
Limited online presence and digital resources compared to larger national nonprofits
No published information about counseling availability (hours, remote options, response times)
best_for: Rural New York homeowners seeking free HUD-certified housing or financial counseling in Delaware, Greene, or Schoharie counties
Low-income seniors and disabled individuals looking for affordable housing with wraparound community support
Small business owners and entrepreneurs in underserved rural communities needing training and commercial space assistance
Households seeking housing rehabilitation grants and grant administration guidance in the service area
diagnosis: WCCRC is ideal for residents of rural upstate New York (specific 16 towns listed) seeking free or low-cost HUD-certified financial and housing counseling combined with potential access to housing rehabilitation or affordable units. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—services are unavailable outside their defined service area in Delaware, Greene, and Schoharie counties.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 125 Main St Ste A, Stamford, NY 12167
